conceptClusters.title: Workspace state and HTML purpose
exitEvidence: Learner distinguishes editable source, parsed preview, saved draft, and passing checkpoint under a changed edit.
Learner assigns content and meaning to HTML without using presentation or behavior as a substitute.

conceptClusters.title: Elements and ordinary text
exitEvidence: Learner labels and authors complete normal elements without calling a tag the whole element.
Learner predicts rendered words and DOM text when spaces, indentation, line breaks, or reserved characters change.

conceptClusters.title: Parsed tree and allowed relationships
exitEvidence: Learner derives parent, child, descendant, ancestor, and sibling relationships from parsed structure rather than indentation.
Learner rejects a browser-tolerated relationship when the controlling content model does not allow it.

conceptClusters.title: Paragraphs headings and lists
exitEvidence: Learner encodes prose, line breaks, topic shifts, and heading ranks from meaning with author styles removed.
Learner selects and nests ordered, unordered, or description lists from the relationships among the content.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A transit interruption notice grows through saved checkpoints into a heading-led update with explanatory prose, service impacts, alternatives, and definitions.
activityDesigns.startingState: A verified empty source file, a blank preview, and a transit communication brief are open.
activityDesigns.completionState: The saved notice survives reload, parser and DOM checks, changed service data, style removal, and a learner explanation of its structure.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback names the observed source, tree, preview, persistence, or changed-case result; the unmet concept; why it matters; and one next action.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Three progressive hints move from the content relationship, to the relevant tree boundary, to a smaller parallel example without supplying final source.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A failed checkpoint routes to a smaller transit example, a repaired prediction, and a parallel changed case before the main artifact can advance.

interactions.title: Read the transit workspace
interactions.learnerAction: Locate the editable source, live preview, save indicator, checkpoint result, and transit brief before changing the artifact.
interactions.artifactChange: No artifact change; workspace evidence is located.

evidence.observable: Workspace controls and evidence surfaces are identified by function rather than position.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Misidentification of source, preview, save, or check state.
interactions.correctionRoute: Highlight one state at a time and contrast its update trigger.

interactions.title: Predict the first source change
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the preview and save-state effects of entering the service name as plain source text without markup.
interactions.artifactChange: Plain service-name text is entered but not saved.

evidence.observable: Prediction separates rendered words from unsaved persistence and unmet structure.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Assuming visible plain text is already a verified HTML artifact.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare immediate preview, reload result, and checkpoint result.

interactions.title: Create the notice heading
interactions.learnerAction: Wrap the service name in the heading structure indicated by its role as the page-level notice title.
interactions.artifactChange: The service name becomes the notice heading element.

evidence.observable: Parsed output contains exactly one top-level notice heading with the required text.
evidence.changedCase: The service name changes and the same heading relationship remains valid.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Heading markup chosen for appearance or formed with incomplete tags.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to content role, then label start tag, text, and end tag.

interactions.title: Save and reload the heading
interactions.learnerAction: Save the heading, reload the workspace, and compare restored source with the last passing checkpoint evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: The heading revision becomes the persisted draft.

evidence.observable: Reload restores exact heading source while the checkpoint reports its real structural state.
evidence.changedCase: A punctuation edit is saved and survives a second reload.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Confusing successful save with successful structural verification.
interactions.correctionRoute: Label the save event and check event separately on a timeline.

interactions.title: Classify the update requirements
interactions.learnerAction: Sort service wording, heading meaning, warning color, and automatic countdown requirements among HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
interactions.artifactChange: No artifact change; requirements are classified.

evidence.observable: HTML receives content and semantic structure while style and dynamic behavior remain separate.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Assigning visual color or countdown behavior to HTML element names.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restate what must remain meaningful when style and scripts are absent.

interactions.title: Remove presentation from the notice
interactions.learnerAction: Toggle author presentation off and inspect whether the current heading still communicates the notice title.
interactions.artifactChange: Presentation is disabled temporarily; source is unchanged.

evidence.observable: Heading role remains understandable without color, size, or spacing cues.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Dependence on default appearance as proof of correct heading meaning.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare the same title as plain text and as a heading in the tree.

interactions.title: Add a plain status sentence
interactions.learnerAction: Enter the supplied interruption status as ordinary text after the heading without adding visual-only formatting.
interactions.artifactChange: A plain status sentence follows the notice heading.

evidence.observable: The required words appear in source order without invented presentation semantics.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Adding elements solely to create an unrequested visual treatment.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reduce the requirement to its content and meaning before choosing markup.

interactions.title: Explain the first checkpoint
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why the saved heading passes its role check while the following plain status text still lacks a prose boundary.
interactions.artifactChange: No change; the first checkpoint evidence is interpreted.

evidence.observable: Explanation distinguishes persistence, passing heading evidence, and the next unmet semantic requirement.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A pass-or-fail summary without causal evidence or next requirement.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace each requirement to a separate observed checkpoint result.

interactions.title: Label the heading anatomy
interactions.learnerAction: Mark the complete heading element, both tags, the tag name, and text content in the saved transit source.
interactions.artifactChange: Source annotations identify heading anatomy without editing it.

evidence.observable: Every selected part has the correct boundary and the whole element includes content and both tags.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Using tag, tag name, and element as interchangeable labels.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reconstruct one complete element from its five labeled parts.

interactions.title: Predict a tag-name change
interactions.learnerAction: Predict what changes in meaning, tree, and visible default when the title tag name changes but its words remain constant.
interactions.artifactChange: A temporary comparison changes only the heading tag name.

evidence.observable: Prediction separates unchanged text from changed element role and possible default presentation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Belief that tag names are only styling shortcuts around unchanged meaning.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect source text, tree role, and preview as three separate observations.

interactions.title: Form the status paragraph
interactions.learnerAction: Convert the plain interruption sentence into one complete paragraph element without changing its supplied wording.
interactions.artifactChange: The interruption status becomes a bounded paragraph.

evidence.observable: The tree contains one paragraph with exact status text and complete element boundaries.
evidence.changedCase: A longer status sentence replaces the words without changing the paragraph relationship.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Incomplete paragraph boundaries or alteration of required content.
interactions.correctionRoute: Label start tag, content, and end tag before editing source again.

interactions.title: Trace text into the tree
interactions.learnerAction: Follow the status sentence from source characters to its paragraph text node and then to visible output.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; the paragraph text-node path is highlighted.

evidence.observable: Source text, parsed text node, and rendered words are distinguished and connected.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Treating the visible words as the editable source or element tag.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace a single word through source, tree, and preview in order.

interactions.title: Test repeated source spaces
interactions.learnerAction: Predict visible spacing after repeated spaces are inserted between two words in the status paragraph.
interactions.artifactChange: Repeated source spaces are inserted temporarily.

evidence.observable: Prediction expects ordinary whitespace collapsing while preserving the words.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Expectation that repeated source spaces create layout spacing.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare one, three, and newline-separated source spaces between two words.

interactions.title: Inspect indentation text nodes
interactions.learnerAction: Reformat the heading and paragraph onto indented lines, then inspect which whitespace nodes appear without changing element relationships.
interactions.artifactChange: Source formatting becomes multiline and consistently indented.

evidence.observable: Tree inspection finds formatting whitespace while element parentage stays unchanged.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Belief that editor indentation changes the DOM hierarchy.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare trees before and after whitespace-only source formatting.

interactions.title: Preserve a reserved character
interactions.learnerAction: Enter the required service label containing a reserved character using the provided character-reference reasoning prompt.
interactions.artifactChange: A reserved character appears safely in notice text.

evidence.observable: The DOM text and preview contain the intended character without creating unintended markup.
evidence.changedCase: A second label uses another supplied reserved-character case.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Confusing text characters with markup delimiters in element content.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare intended text with the parser interpretation of the raw delimiter.

interactions.title: Locate a missing boundary
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the tree when the status paragraph end tag is removed from a disposable copy of the transit source.
interactions.artifactChange: A disposable copy omits the paragraph end tag.

evidence.observable: Prediction names the intended boundary, parsed repair, and diagnostic evidence separately.
evidence.changedCase: The omission moves to a second adjacent paragraph and is predicted again.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Assuming readable preview output proves complete element anatomy.
interactions.correctionRoute: Pair each opening tag with its intended closing boundary before tree inspection.

interactions.title: Repair the paragraph boundary
interactions.learnerAction: Restore the missing end tag in the disposable case and verify that intended and parsed paragraph boundaries now agree.
interactions.artifactChange: The disposable paragraph regains a complete boundary.

evidence.observable: Parser evidence clears and the next sibling remains outside the repaired paragraph.
evidence.changedCase: A new sibling sentence is added and remains structurally separate.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Repair based only on preview appearance without checking the parsed boundary.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect the repaired parent and following sibling in the tree.

interactions.title: Explain source formatting choices
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why indentation improves author readability but repeated spaces do not encode the notice structure or visual layout.
interactions.artifactChange: The clean multiline formatting is saved as a checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Explanation separates author formatting, parser relationships, and presentation responsibility.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attributing structural or styling behavior to source whitespace alone.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restate which characters form tags and which whitespace is ordinary text.

interactions.title: Add an impact container tree
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the tree produced by a heading, paragraph, and three-item unordered list before adding the service-impact content.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit yet; the next tree is arranged from the brief.

evidence.observable: Predicted nodes preserve source order and list-item parentage.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Placing list text directly under the list or using indentation as parentage.
interactions.correctionRoute: Match each content unit to its required element and parent.

interactions.title: Create the impact subheading
interactions.learnerAction: Add a subordinate heading for service impacts at the rank implied by the notice title relationship.
interactions.artifactChange: A service-impact subheading follows the status paragraph.

evidence.observable: The subheading is a sibling in source order and one rank below the notice title.
evidence.changedCase: A second peer topic is inserted and receives the same hierarchical rank.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Selecting a heading rank by desired visual size or nesting it in prose.
interactions.correctionRoute: Draw the title-topic outline before choosing the element.

interactions.title: Open the impact list
interactions.learnerAction: Add an unordered list after the impact heading because the affected services form a collection without a required sequence.
interactions.artifactChange: An empty unordered impact list is introduced.

evidence.observable: The list type matches collection meaning and occupies the intended sibling position.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Choosing ordered or unordered structure from default marker preference.
interactions.correctionRoute: Ask whether reordering the services changes their meaning.

interactions.title: Add the first list item
interactions.learnerAction: Place the first affected service inside a list item that is a direct child of the impact list.
interactions.artifactChange: The impact list gains its first valid list item.

evidence.observable: Exactly one list item is a direct list child and contains the supplied service text.
evidence.changedCase: A replacement service name preserves the same direct-child relationship.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Putting raw text or a paragraph directly where a list item is required.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect the allowed direct child and rebuild one item boundary.

interactions.title: Complete three impact items
interactions.learnerAction: Add the remaining two affected services as separate sibling list items without duplicating the first item boundary.
interactions.artifactChange: The impact list now contains three sibling items.

evidence.observable: List cardinality is exactly three and every item is a sibling direct child.
evidence.changedCase: A fourth supplied service is inserted and cardinality updates without malformed nesting.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Nesting later items inside the first item or outside the list.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace the list parent and each direct child in source order.

interactions.title: Name the impact relationships
interactions.learnerAction: Identify the list parent, item children, text descendants, heading sibling, and notice-title ancestor relationships in the parsed tree.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; impact relationships are selected in the tree.

evidence.observable: Each directional relationship matches a concrete parsed node path.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Confusing descendants with direct children or ancestors with parents.
interactions.correctionRoute: Count tree edges between the two named nodes before labeling.

interactions.title: Challenge indentation parentage
interactions.learnerAction: Predict whether indenting the impact list beneath the paragraph changes their sibling relationship when tag order stays constant.
interactions.artifactChange: Only source indentation changes in a comparison copy.

evidence.observable: Prediction preserves sibling parentage and attributes indentation to author formatting.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Treating visual source indentation as an HTML nesting operator.
interactions.correctionRoute: Remove all indentation and follow only the element boundaries.

interactions.title: Inspect a paragraph-list violation
interactions.learnerAction: Predict author intent and parsed tree when the impact list is placed inside the preceding paragraph in a disposable case.
interactions.artifactChange: A disposable copy nests the list inside a paragraph.

evidence.observable: Prediction identifies the forbidden relationship and likely parser repair without trusting appearance.
evidence.changedCase: The same invalid relationship is tested with a shorter paragraph.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Assuming any visually tolerated parent-child pairing is conforming.
interactions.correctionRoute: Consult the parent content rule and compare authored versus parsed trees.

interactions.title: Verify list content rules
interactions.learnerAction: Run exact DOM relationship and parser checks on the valid impact list, then interpret each observed result.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit unless a relationship check exposes a defect.

evidence.observable: Parser and relationship checks confirm list parentage, item count, and sibling placement.
evidence.changedCase: One new service item is inserted and every relationship check remains valid.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Reading a green aggregate result without understanding checked relationships.
interactions.correctionRoute: Open the first failing relation and locate its two observed nodes.

interactions.title: Nest an alternate stop list
interactions.learnerAction: Add a nested unordered list of alternate stops inside the affected route item that owns those alternatives.
interactions.artifactChange: One impact item gains a nested list of alternate stops.

evidence.observable: Nested list is inside its owning item and its stop items are direct children.
evidence.changedCase: A new alternate stop is added without becoming a peer service impact.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Placing the nested list as a sibling of the owning list item.
interactions.correctionRoute: State which item owns the alternatives, then trace its child boundary.

interactions.title: Trace nested list ancestry
interactions.learnerAction: Trace one alternate stop upward through its item, nested list, owning route item, and outer impact list.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; one complete ancestry path is highlighted.

evidence.observable: The selected node path names every parent and ancestor in correct direction.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Skipping the owning list item or treating both lists as siblings.
interactions.correctionRoute: Move upward one direct parent edge at a time from the text node.

interactions.title: Defend the parsed impact tree
interactions.learnerAction: Explain how tag boundaries and content rules produce the impact tree, including why source indentation alone cannot produce it.
interactions.artifactChange: The verified impact section is saved as a checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Explanation connects boundaries, allowed children, parsed nodes, and observed list relationships.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A tree description that lacks the causal source and content-model rules.
interactions.correctionRoute: Choose one relationship and trace its creating start and end boundaries.

interactions.title: Separate two prose ideas
interactions.learnerAction: Decide whether two supplied interruption updates form one paragraph or two paragraphs, and justify the boundary from prose meaning.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; the prose boundary is decided before authoring.

evidence.observable: Decision uses topic continuity rather than desired vertical spacing.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Choosing paragraph count from line length or visual gap preference.
interactions.correctionRoute: Read each text unit as prose and name its central idea.

interactions.title: Add the cause paragraph
interactions.learnerAction: Author the supplied cause explanation as a complete paragraph after the interruption status.
interactions.artifactChange: A cause paragraph becomes a sibling before the impact heading.

evidence.observable: One complete paragraph contains exact cause text at the required sibling position.
evidence.changedCase: The cause wording doubles in length without changing its semantic boundary.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Merging the cause into the heading or impact list for visual proximity.
interactions.correctionRoute: Locate the prose unit and its intended before-after siblings.

interactions.title: Add the rider guidance paragraph
interactions.learnerAction: Author a separate paragraph for rider guidance because it introduces a distinct action-focused prose idea.
interactions.artifactChange: A rider-guidance paragraph follows the cause paragraph.

evidence.observable: Two sibling paragraphs preserve distinct cause and guidance meanings in source order.
evidence.changedCase: An extra guidance sentence joins only the guidance paragraph.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Using one paragraph or repeated breaks to group unrelated prose ideas.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name the purpose of each sentence before choosing its paragraph parent.

interactions.title: Choose a meaningful line break
interactions.learnerAction: Decide where one line break belongs inside the supplied stop-address text without using it to separate paragraphs.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; the address-line relationship is classified.

evidence.observable: The line break marks an intrinsic address line while prose remains one paragraph.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Using line breaks for arbitrary vertical spacing between content blocks.
interactions.correctionRoute: Ask whether a line ending is part of the content or only its appearance.

interactions.title: Author the stop address lines
interactions.learnerAction: Add the stop name and street line inside one paragraph with exactly one meaningful line break.
interactions.artifactChange: The guidance paragraph gains a two-line stop address.

evidence.observable: One paragraph contains the two address text segments separated by one break element.
evidence.changedCase: A longer street line preserves exactly one content-driven break.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Multiple break elements used to manufacture space or split the paragraph.
interactions.correctionRoute: Remove all breaks, restate the intrinsic line boundary, then add one.

interactions.title: Compare break and paragraph trees
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect how one break within a paragraph differs from two sibling paragraph elements in the parsed tree.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; both tree patterns are compared.

evidence.observable: Inspection distinguishes a child break element from boundaries between paragraph siblings.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Treating line breaks and paragraph boundaries as equivalent structures.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace the parent of the break and the parent of each paragraph.

interactions.title: Recognize a topic transition
interactions.learnerAction: Predict whether a supplied shift from current disruption to long-term repairs warrants a thematic break.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; the topic relationship is evaluated.

evidence.observable: Decision identifies a thematic transition rather than a request for decorative line styling.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Selecting a thematic break only to draw a visible horizontal rule.
interactions.correctionRoute: Describe the before-and-after topics without mentioning appearance.

interactions.title: Mark the repair-topic shift
interactions.learnerAction: Insert a thematic break at the approved transition before the long-term repair update.
interactions.artifactChange: A thematic break separates immediate service news from repair context.

evidence.observable: Exactly one thematic break occupies the intended sibling position between topic groups.
evidence.changedCase: A new immediate-update paragraph stays before the same thematic transition.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Placing the break inside prose or adding repeated decorative rules.
interactions.correctionRoute: Locate the sibling boundary between the two complete topic groups.

interactions.title: Test prose under style removal
interactions.learnerAction: Disable author styles and verify that paragraph boundaries, address line, and thematic transition remain interpretable.
interactions.artifactChange: Styles are disabled temporarily for a semantic check.

evidence.observable: Content relationships remain understandable without authored presentation.
evidence.changedCase: The two paragraphs receive substantially longer replacement text.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A prose structure that works only through margins, color, or fixed line length.
interactions.correctionRoute: Read the unstyled sequence and repair the first ambiguous boundary.

interactions.title: Reject repeated spacing breaks
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the semantic and tree problems caused by inserting three line breaks only to enlarge a gap before the impact heading.
interactions.artifactChange: A disposable comparison contains three spacing-only breaks.

evidence.observable: Prediction rejects presentation-driven break repetition and preserves proper block relationships.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Using HTML break count as a visual spacing control.
interactions.correctionRoute: Remove presentation language and identify the actual content boundary.

interactions.title: Repair prose-only spacing markup
interactions.learnerAction: Remove spacing-only break elements from the disposable case while preserving the real address-line break.
interactions.artifactChange: Only the content-driven address break remains.

evidence.observable: Break cardinality and parent relationship match the one intrinsic line boundary.
evidence.changedCase: Another paragraph is inserted without adding spacing-only breaks.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Deleting every break or retaining decorative breaks after correction.
interactions.correctionRoute: Classify each break by a named content relationship before removal.

interactions.title: Explain prose semantics
interactions.learnerAction: Explain the different jobs performed by the notice paragraphs, address break, and thematic break in the current artifact.
interactions.artifactChange: The verified prose structure is saved as a checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Each element choice is tied to a distinct content relationship and observed tree position.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Definitions repeated without reference to the actual transit content.
interactions.correctionRoute: Point to one content boundary and name what would change if removed.

interactions.title: Outline the notice topics
interactions.learnerAction: Arrange the page title, service impacts, rider alternatives, and repair timeline into a content outline before choosing heading ranks.
interactions.artifactChange: No source edit; a four-node content outline is arranged.

evidence.observable: Outline has one page topic and three peer subordinate topics in intended order.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Heading hierarchy inferred from desired font size or source indentation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Ask which topic contains or is peer to each other topic.

interactions.title: Verify the existing ranks
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect the notice-title and service-impact heading ranks against the arranged content outline.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; existing heading nodes are compared with the outline.

evidence.observable: Existing title and topic ranks match their parent-peer relationships in the outline.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Treating heading rank as unrelated to content hierarchy.
interactions.correctionRoute: Map each heading node back to its topic in the outline.

interactions.title: Add the alternatives heading
interactions.learnerAction: Add a heading for rider alternatives at the same rank as the existing service-impact topic.
interactions.artifactChange: A peer rider-alternatives heading follows the impact content.

evidence.observable: The new heading is a complete element and a same-rank peer in source order.
evidence.changedCase: The peer topic order changes and rank remains based on hierarchy.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Skipping a rank or choosing a smaller-looking heading for later content.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare peer topics in the outline before selecting their rank.

interactions.title: Add the repair heading
interactions.learnerAction: Add the repair-timeline heading after the thematic break using the rank determined by the same content outline.
interactions.artifactChange: A repair-timeline heading begins the post-transition topic.

evidence.observable: The heading follows the thematic break and remains a peer of other subordinate topics.
evidence.changedCase: An introductory repair paragraph is inserted without altering heading rank.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Using the thematic break to justify a new page-level heading rank.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate topic transition from topic containment in the outline.

interactions.title: Detect a skipped heading rank
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the navigation and hierarchy problem when one peer topic changes to a rank that skips the established level.
interactions.artifactChange: A disposable copy introduces a skipped-rank heading.

evidence.observable: Prediction identifies hierarchy mismatch without claiming a universal one-h1 rule.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Judging heading correctness only from boldness or size.
interactions.correctionRoute: Read headings as a nested outline with all style removed.

interactions.title: Run heading navigation check
interactions.learnerAction: Traverse the notice by headings and compare the resulting sequence and ranks with the planned content outline.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit unless navigation exposes a rank mismatch.

evidence.observable: Heading sequence, text, and rank support page and peer-topic navigation.
evidence.changedCase: One topic is renamed and navigation evidence updates without hierarchy drift.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A visually plausible heading set that fails structural navigation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair the first mismatch between outline node and heading evidence.

interactions.title: Separate rank from visual size
interactions.learnerAction: Predict whether hierarchy changes when presentation makes a subordinate heading visually larger than the page heading.
interactions.artifactChange: A comparison style reverses the apparent heading sizes.

evidence.observable: Prediction keeps semantic ranks stable and assigns visual size to presentation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Equating larger visual text with a higher semantic heading rank.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect heading elements and outline while ignoring computed size.

interactions.title: Inspect unstyled heading order
interactions.learnerAction: Disable author styles and inspect the complete heading sequence alongside the transit content outline.
interactions.artifactChange: Author styles are disabled for heading inspection.

evidence.observable: All headings remain identifiable and their ranks encode the intended hierarchy.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Hierarchy that depends on CSS classes or visual placement alone.
interactions.correctionRoute: Align each unstyled heading with exactly one outline topic.

interactions.title: Repair the peer heading rank
interactions.learnerAction: Correct the disposable skipped-rank topic so its semantic rank matches its peer topics without changing its words.
interactions.artifactChange: The disposable heading regains the correct peer rank.

evidence.observable: Heading text is preserved while rank and navigation sequence match the outline.
evidence.changedCase: Another peer topic is inserted and uses the same hierarchy rule.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Changing visible words or styles instead of the semantic rank.
interactions.correctionRoute: Hold text constant and change only the element boundary required by hierarchy.

interactions.title: Defend the heading outline
interactions.learnerAction: Defend every heading rank in the notice using topic containment, peer relationships, and navigation evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: The verified heading hierarchy is saved as a checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Defense ties each rank to content hierarchy and addresses the changed visual-size case.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A defense based on typography preference rather than information structure.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to the content outline and name each peer or child relation.

interactions.title: Classify transit collections
interactions.learnerAction: Classify service impacts, evacuation steps, and fare terms as unordered, ordered, or description-list relationships.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; three content sets are classified.

evidence.observable: List selections follow collection, sequence, and term-description meaning.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Choosing list type by preferred markers instead of content relationship.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test whether reordering changes meaning or terms require definitions.

interactions.title: Verify the impact collection
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect the existing service-impact list and explain why its item order is not semantically required.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; impact items are inspected as a collection.

evidence.observable: Explanation distinguishes collection membership from required sequence.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Assuming source order always makes a list ordered.
interactions.correctionRoute: Swap two impacts and decide whether the notice meaning changes.

interactions.title: Open the alternative sequence
interactions.learnerAction: Create an ordered list under rider alternatives because the supplied actions must occur in sequence.
interactions.artifactChange: An empty ordered action list follows the alternatives heading.

evidence.observable: List type and sibling placement match sequential action meaning.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Using an unordered list for instructions whose order changes outcome.
interactions.correctionRoute: State what fails when the supplied actions are reversed.

interactions.title: Add three ordered actions
interactions.learnerAction: Add the three supplied rider actions as direct list-item children in their required order.
interactions.artifactChange: The alternatives list gains three ordered action items.

evidence.observable: Exactly three direct items appear in the required semantic sequence.
evidence.changedCase: A new prerequisite action is inserted first and all later items shift coherently.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Raw text children, malformed item nesting, or altered action order.
interactions.correctionRoute: Map each action to one direct item and compare source order with the brief.

interactions.title: Test order significance
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the practical meaning change if the final rider action moves to the beginning of the ordered sequence.
interactions.artifactChange: A disposable comparison reorders the action items.

evidence.observable: Prediction connects item order to the changed rider outcome.
evidence.changedCase: A different pair of actions is swapped and analyzed independently.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Treating ordered numbering as decorative presentation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Simulate the actions in displayed order and compare outcomes.

interactions.title: Open the fare glossary
interactions.learnerAction: Create a description list for the supplied fare-status terms and their explanatory descriptions.
interactions.artifactChange: An empty fare-status description list is added after guidance.

evidence.observable: Description-list structure is selected for explicit term-description relationships.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Forcing glossary terms into bullets or ordered steps.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify which text names a term and which text explains it.

interactions.title: Add the first fare term
interactions.learnerAction: Add the first supplied fare term and its description using the allowed description-list child relationships.
interactions.artifactChange: The fare glossary gains one term-description pair.

evidence.observable: Term and description occupy valid direct-child positions with exact supplied text.
evidence.changedCase: The description text changes length without changing its relationship.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Wrapping the term-description pair in an invented list-item child.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect the allowed direct children of the description list.

interactions.title: Complete the fare glossary
interactions.learnerAction: Add the remaining fare term and description while preserving the first pair and valid child order.
interactions.artifactChange: The fare glossary contains two complete term-description pairs.

evidence.observable: The parsed list has two terms and two matching descriptions in intended order.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A partially started library closure notice is completed with progressively less guidance and no reuse of transit content or structure.

activityDesigns.hintContract: Hints fade from relationship prompts in the opening steps to learner-requested concept reminders and no structural placement cues at completion.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Failure returns to a different one-concept library example, requires a repaired changed case, then reopens the original checkpoint.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A malformed storm alert is diagnosed from observed source, parser, tree, preview, and persistence evidence rather than rewritten from a model.
activityDesigns.startingState: A saved alert contains stale preview state, malformed element boundaries, misleading indentation, wrong text structures, and invalid list relationships.
activityDesigns.completionState: Each defect has a causal diagnosis, minimal repair, changed-case verification, saved revision, and distinct correction record.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback reports the earliest contradictory observation and asks for a causal hypothesis before naming any relevant rule.
activityDesigns.hintContract: On-request hints reveal evidence location, then relationship type, then a smaller parallel defect; no hint supplies corrected storm source.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Every repair must reproduce the original failure in a disposable case and survive a different mutation before the defect closes.

interactions.title: Predict the intended alert
interactions.learnerAction: Read the storm brief and predict the heading, prose, and list relationships that a correct unstyled alert should expose.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; intended relationships are recorded before inspection.

evidence.observable: Prediction describes meaning and structure without prescribing visual layout.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Debug goal framed as matching the current preview appearance.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restate the storm information hierarchy with styles absent.

interactions.title: Separate stale evidence states
interactions.learnerAction: Compare source revision, live preview, saved draft, and passing checkpoint to locate which storm evidence is stale.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; mismatched revision evidence is labeled.

evidence.observable: The stale checkpoint is distinguished from current parsed output and saved source.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Beginning code repair from evidence tied to another revision.
interactions.correctionRoute: Order the revision events and discard only stale observations.

interactions.title: Reproduce the saved failure
interactions.learnerAction: Reload the saved storm source and rerun the first failing check to establish a reproducible baseline.
interactions.artifactChange: The saved failing revision becomes the controlled debug baseline.

evidence.observable: Reload reproduces the same first failure against the same source revision.
evidence.changedCase: A disposable text-only edit proves the check is bound to the current revision.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Repairing an unreproducible or unsaved preview state.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restore the last saved failing revision and rerun one check.

evidence.observable: Diagnosis separates visual urgency from document meaning and hierarchy.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Using presentation as evidence that semantic structure passes.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect the unstyled heading sequence and tree roles.

evidence.observable: Selection identifies complete intended anatomy and actual parser repair.
evidence.changedCase: A parallel missing boundary is located in another element.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Selecting the visually strange line instead of the causal boundary.
interactions.correctionRoute: Pair each start tag to its intended end tag in source order.

interactions.title: Repair text delimiter parsing

evidence.observable: Parser error clears and DOM text exactly matches the storm brief.
evidence.changedCase: Another route label supplies a different reserved-character case.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Deleting required punctuation or masking the parser symptom with spaces.

evidence.observable: Predicted repair follows tag order rather than misleading indentation.

interactions.title: Repair the crossed tree

evidence.observable: Authored and parsed trees agree on parentage and sibling order.
evidence.changedCase: A new warning sibling is inserted without reopening crossed nesting.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Broad rewrite that hides the causal boundary defect.
interactions.correctionRoute: Revert and change only the first mismatched close boundary.

interactions.title: Find the forbidden list parent

evidence.observable: Diagnosis names the forbidden paragraph-list relationship and observed repair.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Calling a relationship valid because the browser renders all words.

evidence.observable: List parent, preceding paragraph, following heading, and item relationships all pass.
evidence.changedCase: A new paragraph is inserted before the list without invalid ownership.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Content loss or valid list items moved outside their list.

interactions.title: Classify broken prose spacing
interactions.learnerAction: Classify each storm break as intrinsic line content, topic transition, or presentation-only spacing before repair.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; every current break receives a semantic classification.

evidence.observable: Classification distinguishes paragraphs, meaningful line breaks, thematic shifts, and spacing misuse.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Deleting or preserving breaks solely from their visual effect.

evidence.observable: Reloaded artifact and causal report agree with all current parser and behavior evidence.
evidence.changedCase: One repaired relationship is reproduced in a parallel mini-case.
