activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: An independently designed farmers-market information board communicates schedule, vendor categories, arrival steps, and program terms using only first-module HTML.
activityDesigns.startingState: A blank saved file, a realistic market brief, and behavior-based acceptance evidence are provided without starter markup.
activityDesigns.completionState: The learner submits a persisted, unstyled-readable, parser-clean, mutation-resistant board and a concise defense of every structural choice.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Checks expose observed behavior and unmet competencies but never prescribe tag sequences, exact source shape, or the final document tree.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Optional hints ask relationship questions and point to evidence tools; they do not name the final element or placement for the market artifact.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A failure requires a learner-authored diagnosis, a bounded repair, and a parallel market-data mutation before resubmission.

interactions.title: Plan the market information
interactions.learnerAction: Extract page title, prose ideas, topic hierarchy, collections, sequences, and term-description relationships from the market brief.
interactions.artifactChange: A learner-authored semantic content plan is recorded.

evidence.observable: Plan covers every brief requirement without specifying presentation or behavior.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Missing information or visual-layout requirements treated as HTML meaning.
interactions.correctionRoute: Map each brief sentence to one content relationship.

interactions.title: Establish the first saved revision
interactions.learnerAction: Create and save the first market source revision containing only the chosen page title structure.
interactions.artifactChange: A complete market title element becomes the first saved draft.

evidence.observable: Reload restores one page-title heading with learner-selected text from the brief.
evidence.changedCase: The market name changes and persists without hierarchy drift.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Unsaved first revision or title represented only by plain text.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate edit, preview, save, reload, and check events.

interactions.title: Defend the HTML scope
interactions.learnerAction: Identify which market requests belong to later presentation or behavior work and defend their exclusion from this HTML artifact.
interactions.artifactChange: A scope note accompanies the saved source revision.

evidence.observable: Scope keeps all content and meaning while excluding style and dynamic behavior implementation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Dropping required content or inventing HTML solutions for future behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test each request with styles and scripts absent.

interactions.title: Author the opening prose
interactions.learnerAction: Write complete paragraph elements for market purpose and opening conditions using the supplied factual content.
interactions.artifactChange: Two purpose-specific opening paragraphs follow the title.

evidence.observable: Two complete sibling paragraphs contain all required facts without visual-only breaks.
evidence.changedCase: Longer seasonal conditions remain inside their intended paragraph.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Incomplete element boundaries or unrelated prose merged together.
interactions.correctionRoute: Label each prose idea and its complete element anatomy.

interactions.title: Preserve market text characters
interactions.learnerAction: Encode supplied vendor names containing reserved characters so exact intended text appears without accidental markup.
interactions.artifactChange: Vendor names with reserved punctuation become safe text nodes.

evidence.observable: DOM text preserves every intended character and introduces no unintended element.
evidence.changedCase: A new vendor name supplies a different reserved delimiter case.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Deleted punctuation or parser-created accidental boundaries.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare intended characters with the parsed nodes.

interactions.title: Test formatting-only changes
interactions.learnerAction: Reindent and reflow the market source, then verify content words and parsed relationships remain unchanged.
interactions.artifactChange: Source formatting changes without a semantic edit.

evidence.observable: Whitespace-only source edits preserve DOM text and hierarchy.
evidence.changedCase: The source is compacted and expanded again before comparison.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Structure or visual spacing encoded through source whitespace.
interactions.correctionRoute: Diff tag boundaries separately from whitespace characters.

interactions.title: Explain one source-to-text path
interactions.learnerAction: Trace one vendor name from source characters through complete element anatomy and DOM text to visible output.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; a source-to-output trace is attached as evidence.

evidence.observable: Trace distinguishes markup boundaries, element, text node, and rendering.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Element, tag, text node, and visible text conflated.
interactions.correctionRoute: Select one boundary or node at a time in the inspector.

interactions.title: Create the market topic tree
interactions.learnerAction: Add peer topic headings for schedule, vendors, arrival, and assistance according to the learner-authored content plan.
interactions.artifactChange: Four peer topic headings extend the market tree.

evidence.observable: Heading navigation exposes one title and four coherent peer topics.
evidence.changedCase: A new peer topic is inserted from an updated brief.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Heading rank chosen from visual size or inconsistent peer levels.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare every heading to the content plan outline.

interactions.title: Place schedule prose siblings
interactions.learnerAction: Add schedule and weather-policy paragraphs beneath their topic in source order without invalid structural containment.
interactions.artifactChange: Two schedule-related paragraphs occupy valid sibling positions.

evidence.observable: Paragraph boundaries, order, and allowed relationships pass parsed checks.
evidence.changedCase: A third policy paragraph is inserted at the specified sibling point.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Topic prose nested in another paragraph or heading element.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect the intended parent and before-after siblings.

interactions.title: Inspect the authored tree
interactions.learnerAction: Name parent, child, descendant, ancestor, and sibling relationships in the current market document before adding lists.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; five learner-selected relationship paths are recorded.

evidence.observable: Each relationship is correct, directional, and grounded in parsed nodes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Relationship claims inferred from indentation or visual grouping.
interactions.correctionRoute: Count direct tree edges for the first disputed relationship.

interactions.title: Build a valid vendor collection
interactions.learnerAction: Author the required vendor-category collection with valid list items and one nested vendor-name collection.
interactions.artifactChange: Vendor categories and nested names form a valid two-level list.

evidence.observable: Outer and nested lists have correct owning items, direct children, and content.
evidence.changedCase: One vendor moves to another category and ancestry updates.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Nested collection placed outside the category item that owns it.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace each vendor upward to its owning category.

interactions.title: Challenge content-model tolerance
interactions.learnerAction: Run parser and relationship checks after intentionally testing a disposable visually tolerated invalid parent-child case.
interactions.artifactChange: A disposable invalid case is created, observed, and removed.

evidence.observable: Learner distinguishes browser repair from conforming authored structure.
evidence.changedCase: A different forbidden relationship is detected in a parallel fragment.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Treating readable preview output as conformance evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare authored and parsed parentage against the element model.

interactions.title: Defend tree and content rules
interactions.learnerAction: Defend the market tree and one rejected alternative using parsed relationships and allowed-content evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: A relationship defense is attached to the lab evidence.

evidence.observable: Defense cites observed tree paths and the controlling relationship rule.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Defense based on browser tolerance or source appearance.
interactions.correctionRoute: Supply one parsed node path and one rejected parent-child pair.

interactions.title: Structure schedule prose
interactions.learnerAction: Separate market dates, weather rules, and location lines using paragraphs and only content-driven breaks.
interactions.artifactChange: Schedule prose gains semantic boundaries and one intrinsic address break.

evidence.observable: Paragraph and break relationships match the brief with styles removed.
evidence.changedCase: Longer dates and location text preserve semantic boundaries.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Repeated breaks used to manufacture vertical spacing.
interactions.correctionRoute: Classify each boundary as prose, intrinsic line, or presentation.

interactions.title: Mark a topic transition
interactions.learnerAction: Use a thematic break only if the learner's content analysis supports the shift from attendance information to community programs.
interactions.artifactChange: A justified thematic transition is added or explicitly rejected.

evidence.observable: Decision follows topic relationship rather than decorative line preference.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Thematic element used only for visible decoration.
interactions.correctionRoute: Describe the topics on both sides without appearance terms.

interactions.title: Read the board without styles
interactions.learnerAction: Disable author styles and verify that prose boundaries, intrinsic line, and any thematic transition remain understandable.
interactions.artifactChange: Presentation is disabled for independent semantic testing.

evidence.observable: The board's prose relationships remain coherent in unstyled reading order.
evidence.changedCase: Text length changes substantially and the same meaning survives.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Semantics dependent on margins, line wrapping, or color.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair the first ambiguous content boundary in unstyled output.

interactions.title: Finalize heading hierarchy
interactions.learnerAction: Add or revise topic headings so the complete market outline is navigable by heading text and rank alone.
interactions.artifactChange: The final heading sequence matches the content plan.

evidence.observable: Heading navigation reconstructs the title and all topic relationships.
evidence.changedCase: One topic is renamed and repositioned without incorrect rank changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Skipped ranks or heading choices based on visual size.
interactions.correctionRoute: Align each heading with one node in the original outline.

interactions.title: Defend heading ranks
interactions.learnerAction: Defend every market heading rank under a comparison style that reverses their apparent visual sizes.
interactions.artifactChange: A heading-rank defense joins the evidence record.

evidence.observable: Defense relies on hierarchy and navigation rather than size or boldness.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual presentation used as semantic-rank evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use only the content outline and heading navigation output.

interactions.title: Author vendor categories
interactions.learnerAction: Choose and author the appropriate list relationship for vendor categories whose order does not change meaning.
interactions.artifactChange: Vendor categories form an unordered semantic collection.

evidence.observable: Collection type, exact membership, and direct-child relationships pass.
evidence.changedCase: Categories are reordered without changing semantic meaning.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Ordered structure chosen because numbers look preferable.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test whether category reordering changes the brief's meaning.

interactions.title: Author arrival procedure
interactions.learnerAction: Choose and author the list relationship for arrival actions whose sequence affects successful entry.
interactions.artifactChange: Arrival actions form a correctly ordered procedure.

evidence.observable: Required action order and valid item parentage are preserved.
evidence.changedCase: A new first prerequisite shifts later steps coherently.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Unordered procedure or item order copied incorrectly.
interactions.correctionRoute: Simulate the supplied actions in source order.

interactions.title: Author program definitions
interactions.learnerAction: Choose and author term-description relationships for assistance programs and eligibility explanations.
interactions.artifactChange: Assistance programs form a valid description list.

evidence.observable: Every program term controls its intended description with valid direct children.
evidence.changedCase: One term receives a second description without mispairing the next term.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Definitions represented as unrelated bullets or invalid wrappers.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace each description to its preceding controlling term.

interactions.title: Mutate the market lists
interactions.learnerAction: Add a vendor, reorder arrival steps, and lengthen one definition while observing which invariants pass or fail.
interactions.artifactChange: Three list mutations exercise collection, sequence, and definition meaning.

evidence.observable: Valid collection and definition changes pass while harmful procedure reordering is detected.
evidence.changedCase: Different content values repeat the three mutation classes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Checks tied to exact tags without semantic behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name each list's invariant before reading results.

interactions.title: Defend list relationships
interactions.learnerAction: Defend list types, item ownership, nesting, and order behavior for all market collections and procedures.
interactions.artifactChange: A list-design defense accompanies behavior evidence.

evidence.observable: Defense cites meaning, parsed relationships, and changed-case results.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Defense based on marker appearance or memorized syntax.
interactions.correctionRoute: For each list, state the consequence of reordering or regrouping.

interactions.title: Run the integrated gate
interactions.learnerAction: Save, reload, run parser, exact DOM, relationship, heading-navigation, and unstyled-reading checks against the complete board.
interactions.artifactChange: The complete board becomes a persisted candidate submission.

evidence.observable: Current saved revision passes parser and behavior evidence without stale state.
evidence.changedCase: A market-date edit survives reload and all unaffected checks.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Submission evidence tied to an unsaved or stale revision.
interactions.correctionRoute: Find the earliest revision mismatch and rerun from that state.

interactions.title: Run adversarial structure cases
interactions.learnerAction: Insert a peer paragraph, move a nested vendor, and test a disposable invalid parent to verify relationship checks.
interactions.artifactChange: Three structural mutations challenge the candidate submission.

evidence.observable: Valid changes preserve invariants and invalid parentage produces actionable evidence.
evidence.changedCase: A second invalid relation is rejected without altering the saved board.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Structure passing only original source shape or item counts.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate content values from the relationship invariant under test.

interactions.title: Run adversarial meaning cases
interactions.learnerAction: Lengthen prose, reverse visual heading sizes, reorder a procedure, and add a definition while testing semantic behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: Four meaning mutations exercise the full market artifact.

evidence.observable: Semantic boundaries and hierarchy survive valid changes while wrong order is detected.
evidence.changedCase: A parallel data set repeats all four mutation classes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Meaning that depends on fixed text, visual size, or original order.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair the first violated semantic invariant, then repeat its mutation.

interactions.title: Submit and defend the board
interactions.learnerAction: Submit the market board and defend its workspace history, boundaries, tree, content rules, prose, hierarchy, lists, correction, and mutations.
interactions.artifactChange: The board and evidence package become the independent-lab submission.

evidence.observable: Defense supports every module concept with artifact-specific observed evidence.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Completion assertion unsupported by behavior, correction, or transfer evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reopen the weakest competency claim and demonstrate it on a mutation.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A mixed retrieval set reconnects source, tree, preview, persistence, semantic decisions, and prior correction without reopening completed worked examples.
activityDesigns.startingState: No prior artifact is visible; short unfamiliar fragments and evidence traces appear one at a time.
activityDesigns.completionState: The learner retrieves every module concept, corrects two errors, and schedules missed concepts for adaptive parallel review.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Answers are committed before feedback; feedback contrasts the response with observed evidence and identifies the next retrieval target.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Hints are unavailable before the first response and afterward offer only concept cues or a smaller parallel retrieval item.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Every miss requires explanation, a parallel item, and a delayed reappearance; rereading alone cannot close review evidence.

interactions.title: Retrieve four workspace states
interactions.learnerAction: From an unfamiliar event trace, identify editable source, parsed preview, saved draft, and passing checkpoint revisions.
interactions.artifactChange: No artifact change; a retrieval response is recorded.

evidence.observable: All four states are matched to revision evidence rather than visual position.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Preview, saved, and passing state conflated.
interactions.correctionRoute: Complete a parallel event-order trace, then schedule delayed retrieval.

interactions.title: Predict save and reload behavior
interactions.learnerAction: Predict source and preview after an edit, live parse, reload, save, and second reload in the supplied order.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; predicted revision states are ordered.

evidence.observable: Prediction identifies which exact edit persists at both reload points.
evidence.changedCase: A parallel trace moves the save event before the first reload.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Ignoring event order when predicting persistence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Mark each event's input and output revision before retrying.

interactions.title: Retrieve language boundaries
interactions.learnerAction: Classify five unfamiliar content, meaning, presentation, and dynamic requirements among HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; requirement classifications are committed.

evidence.observable: HTML receives content and semantics while style and response behavior stay separate.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visible output assumed to make every requirement HTML.
interactions.correctionRoute: Retry with the same content under changed style and behavior.

interactions.title: Retrieve complete element anatomy
interactions.learnerAction: Label the whole element, start tag, tag name, content, and end tag in valid and malformed unfamiliar examples.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; anatomy labels and malformed boundary are recorded.

evidence.observable: Labels preserve exact boundaries and identify the malformed component.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Opening tag or name labeled as the complete element.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild one parallel element from labeled parts.

interactions.title: Predict text and whitespace
interactions.learnerAction: Predict DOM text and visible spacing for an unfamiliar fragment with indentation, repeated spaces, line breaks, and reserved characters.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; text-node and rendering predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Prediction distinguishes source formatting, parsed text, and intended reserved characters.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Literal rendering predicted for every whitespace character.
interactions.correctionRoute: Retry with two words and one controlled character change.

interactions.title: Reconstruct a parsed tree
interactions.learnerAction: Arrange the tree for a nested unfamiliar fragment before revealing browser parser evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; a predicted tree is committed.

evidence.observable: Tree follows boundaries, source order, and text children rather than indentation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Nodes placed by source formatting or visual output.
interactions.correctionRoute: Process open and close boundaries sequentially in a parallel fragment.

interactions.title: Name directional relationships
interactions.learnerAction: Name parent, child, descendant, ancestor, preceding sibling, and following sibling pairs in the reconstructed tree.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; relationship paths are committed.

evidence.observable: Each relationship is directional and matches the selected parsed nodes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Parent confused with ancestor or sibling direction reversed.
interactions.correctionRoute: Count tree edges and retry one relationship pair.

interactions.title: Reject tolerated invalid content
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why a browser-rendered unfamiliar relationship is nonconforming and predict the parser-created correction.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; a content-model judgment is recorded.

evidence.observable: Explanation cites forbidden parentage and distinguishes authored from parsed tree.
evidence.changedCase: A second visually tolerated invalid relation is analyzed.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visible output used as conformance proof.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect actual parentage and the controlling allowed-content rule.

interactions.title: Retrieve prose element choices
interactions.learnerAction: Choose paragraph, line break, thematic break, or no new boundary for four unfamiliar content relationships.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; four semantic choices are committed.

evidence.observable: Choices follow prose idea, intrinsic line, and topic-transition meaning.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Break elements chosen from desired visual spacing.
interactions.correctionRoute: Retry after describing each content relationship without appearance terms.

interactions.title: Retrieve heading hierarchy
interactions.learnerAction: Assign heading ranks to an unfamiliar title and topic outline after all visual size cues are removed.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; rank assignments are committed.

evidence.observable: Ranks reflect title, child, and peer topic relationships without universal myths.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Rank chosen from display size or source order alone.
interactions.correctionRoute: Arrange outline containment before retrying ranks.

interactions.title: Retrieve list relationships
interactions.learnerAction: Select list types and direct-child patterns for a collection, procedure, glossary, and nested subcollection.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; four list designs are committed.

evidence.observable: Selections preserve order significance, term pairing, and nested ownership.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All cases forced into identical list-item structure.
interactions.correctionRoute: Ask whether order, definition, or ownership changes meaning.

interactions.title: Correct a malformed fragment
interactions.learnerAction: Diagnose and minimally repair one unfamiliar missing boundary and one forbidden list relationship.
interactions.artifactChange: A temporary fragment becomes parser-clean and structurally valid.

evidence.observable: Minimal repairs align authored and parsed trees without unrelated rewrite.
evidence.changedCase: A sibling insertion and longer text preserve both repairs.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Symptom masking, broad rewrite, or indentation-only correction.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name the first causal boundary or relationship before re-editing.

interactions.title: Correct semantic structure drift
interactions.learnerAction: Repair a fragment that uses spacing breaks, visual heading ranks, and the wrong list type while preserving all words.
interactions.artifactChange: Semantic boundaries, hierarchy, and list meaning are corrected.

evidence.observable: Unstyled navigation and reordered-content checks demonstrate all three repairs.
evidence.changedCase: New text and list items preserve the corrected semantic rules.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Word or style changes substituted for structural correction.
interactions.correctionRoute: Hold content constant and repair one semantic relationship at a time.

interactions.title: Explain and schedule correction
interactions.learnerAction: Explain the strongest and weakest retrieval evidence, record the corrected misconception, and schedule a parallel delayed review.
interactions.artifactChange: Review evidence and adaptive next-review targets are persisted.

evidence.observable: Saved review record links misses, corrections, parallel success, and due concepts.
evidence.changedCase: A later parallel item must be answerable without reopening this review.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Review marked complete from exposure rather than corrected retrieval.
interactions.correctionRoute: Keep the missed concept due until a parallel retrieval succeeds.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A secure first-content performance bank samples prediction, code reading, debugging, changed cases, independent construction, and defense across unfamiliar contexts.
activityDesigns.startingState: No instructional artifact, worked solution, hint, or correctness feedback is available during the scored attempt.
activityDesigns.completionState: Server-canonical evidence records item-level competency results, uncertainty, correction needs, and an unscored parallel reassessment path.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: During scoring only submission receipt is shown; after completion feedback reports observed evidence and competency, never canonical source or hidden answers.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Hints are unavailable in every scored interaction; requesting instruction exits the scored attempt and routes to remediation.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Missed competencies route to a smaller model, faded parallel practice, and a different secured reassessment item before mastery can change.

interactions.title: Analyze an edit-state trace
interactions.learnerAction: Determine which source revision appears after two edits, one save, one failed check, and a reload in an unfamiliar workspace trace.
interactions.artifactChange: A scored state-sequence response is submitted.

evidence.observable: Response identifies restored source and separates save from passing evidence.
evidence.changedCase: A parallel form changes save and reload order.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Incorrect revision or conflated saved and passing state.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to event-sequence model and a new parallel trace.

interactions.title: Predict source and preview drift
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the visible output, saved draft, and checkpoint status after an unsaved valid edit followed by a failing structural edit.
interactions.artifactChange: A three-state prediction is submitted.

evidence.observable: Prediction assigns each revision to the correct source, preview, and evidence state.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Live output treated as durable or automatically passing.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to a smaller source-preview-persistence contrast.

interactions.title: Classify implementation responsibility
interactions.learnerAction: Classify unfamiliar content, semantic, visual, and interactive requirements across HTML, CSS, and JavaScript with one justification.
interactions.artifactChange: Responsibility choices and justification are submitted.

evidence.observable: HTML choices are limited to content and meaning and justification survives style removal.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Presentation or behavior incorrectly assigned to HTML.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to boundary classification with changed presentation.

interactions.title: Defend an unstyled document
interactions.learnerAction: Defend whether an unfamiliar unstyled fragment still communicates its intended information hierarchy and identify one missing HTML relationship.
interactions.artifactChange: A scored semantic defense is submitted.

evidence.observable: Defense cites content relationships instead of default visual appearance.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Defense relies on color, size, position, or scripting.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to unstyled contrast and parallel defense prompt.

interactions.title: Label unfamiliar element anatomy
interactions.learnerAction: Label complete element, start tag, name, content, and end tag in an unfamiliar valid source fragment.
interactions.artifactChange: Five scored source ranges are submitted.

evidence.observable: Every label selects the exact intended boundary and distinguishes part from whole.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Tag name, start tag, or content mislabeled as the element.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to anatomy reconstruction with a different element.

interactions.title: Diagnose an incomplete element
interactions.learnerAction: Identify the causal missing boundary in unfamiliar malformed source and state the smallest valid repair without editing unrelated content.
interactions.artifactChange: A causal diagnosis and repair location are submitted.

evidence.observable: Diagnosis matches parser evidence and restores intended element extent.
evidence.changedCase: A parallel form omits a different normal-element boundary.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Symptom line selected instead of causal boundary.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to tag-pair tracing and a different malformed fragment.

interactions.title: Author complete bounded prose
interactions.learnerAction: Author a complete element for supplied prose in a blank fragment without copied starter syntax.
interactions.artifactChange: A scored source fragment is submitted.

evidence.observable: Parsed output has one complete prose element with exact required text.
evidence.changedCase: Server validation substitutes longer prose while keeping the requirement.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Incomplete boundary, extra wrapper, or altered required text.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to faded element construction with changed prose.

interactions.title: Predict whitespace rendering
interactions.learnerAction: Predict DOM text and visible spacing for unfamiliar source containing indentation, repeated spaces, and line breaks.
interactions.artifactChange: DOM-text and rendering predictions are submitted.

evidence.observable: Prediction distinguishes formatting whitespace, collapsed output, and element boundaries.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Every source whitespace character predicted as literal output.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to a two-word controlled whitespace comparison.

interactions.title: Preserve reserved text
interactions.learnerAction: Author required text containing reserved delimiters so parsed text exactly matches the supplied label without extra elements.
interactions.artifactChange: A reserved-character source fragment is submitted.

evidence.observable: DOM text is exact and parser output contains no unintended markup.
evidence.changedCase: Server validation supplies a second reserved-character label.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Punctuation removed or interpreted as unintended markup.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to delimiter-versus-text parsing practice.

interactions.title: Construct a parsed tree
interactions.learnerAction: Arrange the parsed tree for an unfamiliar nested fragment that includes element and text nodes.
interactions.artifactChange: A scored tree arrangement is submitted.

evidence.observable: Tree preserves boundary-derived nesting, node order, and text children.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Tree constructed from indentation or visible layout.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to sequential boundary tracing on a parallel fragment.

interactions.title: Identify tree relationships
interactions.learnerAction: Identify directional parent, descendant, ancestor, and sibling relationships among selected nodes in an unfamiliar tree.
interactions.artifactChange: Four scored relationship selections are submitted.

evidence.observable: All relationship names, directions, and directness match the parsed paths.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Direct and transitive relationships or directions confused.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to edge-counting relationship practice.

interactions.title: Repair crossed nesting
interactions.learnerAction: Diagnose and minimally repair crossed close order while preserving all supplied words and intended sibling order.
interactions.artifactChange: A repaired scored fragment is submitted.

evidence.observable: Authored and parsed tree agree after a minimal causal boundary repair.
evidence.changedCase: Server inserts a new sibling to verify durable nesting.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Broad rewrite, content loss, or indentation-only repair.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to paired-boundary diagnosis with another close-order defect.

interactions.title: Judge allowed parentage
interactions.learnerAction: Judge multiple unfamiliar parent-child relationships as allowed or forbidden and identify the controlling content relationship.
interactions.artifactChange: Relationship judgments and reasons are submitted.

evidence.observable: Judgments follow author requirements rather than browser tolerance or appearance.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Rendered output used as the only validity test.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to authored-versus-parsed relationship comparison.

interactions.title: Repair tolerated invalid content
interactions.learnerAction: Repair a visually readable but forbidden paragraph-list relationship while preserving prose, list membership, and source order.
interactions.artifactChange: A conforming scored fragment is submitted.

evidence.observable: Parser and exact relationship checks confirm valid siblings and unchanged content.
evidence.changedCase: Server adds another paragraph and list item before rechecking.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Appearance-only fix or list content loss.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to subtree movement with a parallel invalid parent.

interactions.title: Choose prose boundaries
interactions.learnerAction: Choose paragraphs, a meaningful line break, a thematic break, or no boundary for unfamiliar prose and topic cases.
interactions.artifactChange: Four scored semantic choices are submitted.

evidence.observable: Choices distinguish prose ideas, intrinsic lines, topic shifts, and presentation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Break choices based on desired spacing or line wrapping.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to content-relation classification with new cases.

interactions.title: Repair break misuse
interactions.learnerAction: Repair spacing-only break repetition while preserving one intrinsic line break and one genuine topic transition.
interactions.artifactChange: A semantically corrected prose fragment is submitted.

evidence.observable: Paragraph, line-break, and thematic-break relationships match content roles.
evidence.changedCase: Server lengthens prose and changes the intrinsic line text.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All breaks retained, removed, or converted without semantic analysis.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to boundary classification and a different prose repair.

interactions.title: Defend changed prose structure
interactions.learnerAction: Defend paragraph and break choices after the assessment changes text length and disables all author styles.
interactions.artifactChange: A scored changed-case defense is submitted.

evidence.observable: Defense cites prose meaning and intrinsic lines, not wrapping or margins.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Fixed-length or presentation-based boundary rationale.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to unstyled prose comparison and parallel defense.

interactions.title: Assign unfamiliar heading ranks
interactions.learnerAction: Assign ranks to an unfamiliar page title, child topics, and peer subtopics from a supplied content outline.
interactions.artifactChange: A scored heading outline is submitted.

evidence.observable: Ranks encode containment and peer relationships without appearance cues.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Skipped ranks or visual-size reasoning.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to outline-first heading practice with new topics.

interactions.title: Repair heading hierarchy drift
interactions.learnerAction: Repair a title-topic hierarchy with one inverted rank and one skipped peer while preserving all words.
interactions.artifactChange: A corrected scored heading fragment is submitted.

evidence.observable: Heading navigation matches the supplied content outline under style removal.
evidence.changedCase: Server inserts another peer topic and reverses visual sizes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Styling or word changes substituted for rank correction.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to tag-boundary repair against a parallel outline.

interactions.title: Defend hierarchy under style reversal
interactions.learnerAction: Defend heading ranks after a comparison view makes subordinate headings visually larger than the page title.
interactions.artifactChange: A scored hierarchy defense is submitted.

evidence.observable: Defense uses content containment and navigation evidence despite presentation reversal.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Rank justified by size, boldness, or screen position.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to unstyled heading navigation and new defense prompt.

interactions.title: Select list semantics
interactions.learnerAction: Select list types for an unfamiliar collection, required procedure, and term-definition set and justify each choice.
interactions.artifactChange: Three scored list choices and reasons are submitted.

evidence.observable: Choices follow order significance and term-description meaning rather than markers.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Identical list type selected for all content relationships.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to reorder-and-pair meaning comparisons.

interactions.title: Build three list models
interactions.learnerAction: Author small unordered, ordered, and description lists from unfamiliar supplied content with valid direct children.
interactions.artifactChange: Three scored list fragments are submitted.

evidence.observable: List types, direct-child models, content, and required order all pass.
evidence.changedCase: Server changes membership, step count, and description length.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One memorized list shape copied across distinct models.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to faded construction of the missed list model.

interactions.title: Repair nested list ownership
interactions.learnerAction: Repair a nested collection that is currently a peer of the item whose content it qualifies.
interactions.artifactChange: A repaired scored nested-list fragment is submitted.

evidence.observable: Nested list ancestry, direct items, and outer peer order match intended ownership.
evidence.changedCase: Server moves one nested item and adds an outer peer.

interactions.feedbackTarget: List type changed without repairing ownership or boundaries.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to ancestry tracing with a different nested collection.

interactions.title: Diagnose an integrated defect set
interactions.learnerAction: Find the root causes of malformed boundary, invalid parentage, misleading indentation, and incorrect list ownership in one fragment.
interactions.artifactChange: Four causal diagnoses are submitted before any repair.

evidence.observable: Diagnoses distinguish source formatting from causal boundaries and relationship rules.
evidence.changedCase: A parallel form changes defect locations and content values.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Symptoms listed without root causes or parser evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route each missed cause to its smallest debugging clinic.

interactions.title: Build an unfamiliar notice
interactions.learnerAction: Build and save a compact public notice from a new brief using complete elements, meaningful prose, coherent headings, and appropriate lists.
interactions.artifactChange: A scored independent notice and saved revision are submitted.

evidence.observable: Reloaded artifact passes server-canonical parser, DOM, relationship, and semantic checks.
evidence.changedCase: Server supplies changed title, prose length, list membership, and topic order.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Copied source shape, unsaved result, or structure overfit to original values.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route failed concepts to separate remediation before a new build brief.

interactions.title: Repair the changed notice
interactions.learnerAction: Repair the independent notice after changed content exposes one prose, hierarchy, or list invariant failure.
interactions.artifactChange: A repaired changed-case notice is submitted.

evidence.observable: Repair restores the violated semantic invariant without breaking unaffected evidence.
evidence.changedCase: A different server-selected mutation verifies the same rule.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Reversion to original values or broad rewrite instead of invariant repair.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route to causal correction practice and a different mutation.

interactions.title: Defend independent mastery evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Defend the saved notice, one debugging decision, one changed-case result, and the boundary between demonstrated and undemonstrated skill.
interactions.artifactChange: A scored evidence defense closes the performance attempt.

evidence.observable: Defense cites artifact-specific behavior, acknowledges limits, and does not rely on completion count.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Mastery claim based on points, step count, or unsupported confidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Route unsupported claims to parallel evidence tasks, never answer review.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A worked public-service notice evolves from plain source characters into a saved, inspectable heading, prose block, and list.
activityDesigns.startingState: An unsaved text-only notice and an empty preview are visible side by side.
activityDesigns.completionState: The learner has predicted, inspected, and explained the saved source, parsed tree, visible preview, and semantic choices.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Each response contrasts the learner prediction with observed source, tree, preview, or saved-state evidence and names the controlling idea.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Worked guidance reveals one reasoning move at a time but pauses for a prediction before displaying the observed result.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: An incorrect prediction returns to the smallest contrasting example, asks for a revised prediction, and then rechecks a changed example.

interactions.title: Separate four workspace states
interactions.learnerAction: Identify which pane is editable source, parsed preview, saved draft state, and last passing checkpoint before any edit.
interactions.artifactChange: No artifact change; workspace states are labeled.

evidence.observable: All four states are matched to their actual role without treating preview as source.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Confusion between editable, parsed, saved, and passing state.
interactions.correctionRoute: Replay the four-state contrast with one stale preview state.

interactions.title: Predict a first visible edit
interactions.learnerAction: Predict exactly which preview words change when one bounded phrase changes in source while the draft remains unsaved.
interactions.artifactChange: One source phrase changes without saving the draft.

evidence.observable: Prediction separates immediate parsed output from persistence after reload.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Assumption that rendered output and persisted source are identical states.
interactions.correctionRoute: Contrast a live preview update with a reload of the unsaved draft.

interactions.title: Observe save and checkpoint drift
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect an edit that is saved but does not yet pass and explain why saved state and verified state show different revisions.
interactions.artifactChange: The changed phrase is saved while its check remains failing.

evidence.observable: Reload preserves the edited phrase while checkpoint evidence still names the unmet behavior.
evidence.changedCase: A second valid phrase is saved and checked independently.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Belief that saving an artifact proves that its requirements pass.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to the saved-versus-passing timeline and label each event.

interactions.title: Assign web language responsibility
interactions.learnerAction: Classify changed requirements for words, meaning, visual presentation, and dynamic response as HTML, CSS, or JavaScript responsibilities.
interactions.artifactChange: No change; four requirements are placed by responsibility.

evidence.observable: Content and semantic requirements map to HTML without treating tag names as visual commands.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Language-boundary errors between structure, presentation, and behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use one requirement whose wording stays constant while style changes.

interactions.title: Test meaning without author style
interactions.learnerAction: Compare styled and unstyled copies of a notice and explain which content relationships must remain understandable in HTML alone.
interactions.artifactChange: Author styling is toggled off for the worked notice.

evidence.observable: Explanation names persistent content meaning rather than describing only appearance.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Use of visual size or color as the only source of document meaning.
interactions.correctionRoute: Show the same HTML under a different stylesheet and ask what survives.

interactions.title: Locate a complete element
interactions.learnerAction: Select the entire heading element in source, then separately select its start tag, tag name, text content, and end tag.
interactions.artifactChange: The worked heading source receives anatomy annotations.

evidence.observable: Selections distinguish the whole element from each tag and from its content.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Calling an opening tag or tag name the complete HTML element.
interactions.correctionRoute: Collapse the example to one element and rebuild its labeled parts.

interactions.title: Trace matching tag boundaries
interactions.learnerAction: Predict which content belongs to each of two adjacent elements when their matching start and end tags are highlighted independently.
interactions.artifactChange: A paragraph is added after the worked heading.

evidence.observable: Each content span is bounded by the correct matching tag pair.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Reading all following text as part of the first opened element.
interactions.correctionRoute: Pair each start tag to its end tag before reconsidering content spans.

interactions.title: Predict a missing end tag
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the intended and browser-parsed result when a normal element loses its end tag, then compare both against the tree.
interactions.artifactChange: A copy of the paragraph temporarily loses its end tag.

evidence.observable: Prediction distinguishes author intent from the browser-created tree and parser evidence.
evidence.changedCase: The missing end tag moves to the adjacent element and is predicted again.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Assuming visible text proves that malformed element boundaries are correct.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare the intended tag pairs with the parsed tree one boundary at a time.

interactions.title: Predict collapsed source spacing
interactions.learnerAction: Predict visible spacing after repeated spaces, indentation, and source line breaks are introduced around ordinary notice words.
interactions.artifactChange: Source whitespace changes while the notice words remain identical.

evidence.observable: Prediction identifies collapsed spacing without claiming indentation creates structure.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Expectation that every source space and line break renders literally.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reduce the case to two words with controlled whitespace changes.

interactions.title: Find text nodes in the tree
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect which words and formatting whitespace become text nodes between the worked heading and paragraph elements.
interactions.artifactChange: No source change; text nodes are exposed in the tree view.

evidence.observable: Inspection distinguishes element nodes, meaningful text, and formatting whitespace.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Belief that the parsed document contains only element tags.
interactions.correctionRoute: Toggle text-node visibility and trace one word from source to tree.

interactions.title: Build a tree from nested source
interactions.learnerAction: Arrange a small tree for a list nested after notice prose before the browser tree is revealed.
interactions.artifactChange: A three-item list is added to the worked notice source.

evidence.observable: The arranged tree preserves nesting, order, and distinct text children.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Tree construction based on visual indentation instead of tag boundaries.
interactions.correctionRoute: Match open and close boundaries before placing each child node.

interactions.title: Name document relationships
interactions.learnerAction: Identify parent, child, descendant, ancestor, and adjacent sibling pairs in the parsed notice tree.
interactions.artifactChange: No change; relationship paths are highlighted in the tree.

evidence.observable: Every relationship is directional and grounded in the actual parsed nodes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Interchanging parent, ancestor, child, descendant, and sibling terms.
interactions.correctionRoute: Walk one node edge at a time and restate relationship direction.

interactions.title: Challenge misleading indentation
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the parsed parent when indentation suggests one relationship but the closing tags create another.
interactions.artifactChange: Indentation changes without changing any tag boundary.

evidence.observable: Prediction follows tag boundaries and explains why source spacing is not structure.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Treating indentation as a parser instruction for parentage.
interactions.correctionRoute: Remove indentation entirely and trace only the opening and closing order.

interactions.title: Reject a tolerated relationship
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why a list directly inside a paragraph is not an allowed author relationship even when the preview appears readable.
interactions.artifactChange: A nonconforming paragraph-list example is inspected separately.

evidence.observable: Explanation cites the parent-child rule and browser repair instead of appearance.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Using browser tolerance or visible output as conformance proof.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare authored nesting with the repaired tree and controlling model.

interactions.title: Choose prose boundaries
interactions.learnerAction: Choose paragraphs, one meaningful line break, or a thematic break for changed prose, address-line, and topic-shift requirements.
interactions.artifactChange: The worked notice gains prose, one address line, and a topic transition.

evidence.observable: Each chosen element follows the content relationship rather than desired spacing.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Using repeated line breaks as a substitute for paragraph or topic structure.
interactions.correctionRoute: Disable styles and restate the relationship between adjacent text units.

interactions.title: Rank headings from hierarchy
interactions.learnerAction: Select heading ranks for the notice title and two subordinate topics after visual size cues are removed.
interactions.artifactChange: Two subordinate topic headings join the notice title.

evidence.observable: Ranks encode the content hierarchy without relying on default font size.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Choosing heading rank to obtain a preferred visual size.
interactions.correctionRoute: Convert the content into an outline before assigning heading ranks.

interactions.title: Select list meaning
interactions.learnerAction: Select ordered, unordered, or description list structure for steps, available routes, and term-definition content.
interactions.artifactChange: Three alternate list structures are compared outside the saved notice.

evidence.observable: Selections follow sequence, collection, or term-description relationships.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Treating list types as interchangeable bullet styles.
interactions.correctionRoute: Ask whether order changes meaning or whether terms require descriptions.

interactions.title: Explain the complete notice
interactions.learnerAction: Explain how source boundaries, parsed relationships, heading hierarchy, prose, and list choice preserve notice meaning after reload.
interactions.artifactChange: The completed worked notice is saved as its final model checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Reloaded artifact, parsed tree, and explanation agree on every named relationship.
evidence.changedCase: One route item is inserted and the learner updates the explanation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Disconnected facts that do not explain the integrated artifact behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace one content requirement through source, tree, preview, save, and check.

authorshipBoundary.interpretation: This artifact fixes interaction intent and evidence before authoring; it is neither publishable learner content nor a source for automatic prose generation.

gaps: Independent subject, instructional-design, assessment, accessibility, and duplication reviews are not yet complete.
Learner-facing copy, starter states, canonical server answers, executable checks, and observed pilot evidence remain unauthored.
